recipient.name: Drew Onuska
recipient.title: HR Operations Program Manager at OHSU
recipient.degree: MS, Human Resource Management — Purdue University

outreach.linkedin_note: Hi Drew — I just applied to the HR Business Partner role at OHSU and wanted to reach out. I'm a Portland-local Organizational Psychology and HRBP professional, and I'd love to connect with someone doing HR program work at OHSU.

email.subject: Portland HRBP applicant — drawn to the People work at OHSU
email.body:
Hi Drew,

I recently applied to the HR Business Partner role at OHSU, and I wanted to reach out personally since you're doing HR program work there. As a Portland local, OHSU has been on my radar for a while, and the HRBP role lined up closely with how I like to work.

My background is on the strategic side of People — organizational development consulting, HRBP support, and program management. I have an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology, and most of my work has been org design, change management, and partnering with leaders on talent and engagement. One project I'm proud of: an OD diagnosis that surfaced $362K in avoidable leave costs and reframed how leadership thought about it.

I tend to be drawn to organizations where People is treated as a strategic lever rather than a purely operational function — and from the outside, it sounds like that's the case at OHSU. If you're open to it, I'd love to hear how you see the People team's role there. I'm flexible over the next two weeks and happy to work around your schedule.

Warmly,
Jamie (Yi-Chieh) Cheng
jamiecheng0103@gmail.com
